So many students have the same problem, and me to. 
    In first semester it was wery big problem for me. But now I have some ideas how can I fix it. I will give you them.

1) You need to have some task-management system. In that system you can prioritize your tasks.

2) You study in uni, so you have some classes. Use "Google Calendar" to manage them.

3) Combine similar tasks - then you will do all tasks faster.

4) Remember to get enouth sleep and to do sport well, cause it can bust your productivity.

    I hope that some of my tips can help you.", "strength_type": "Text organization", "note": "Clear structure with numbered points and logical flow."}], "feedback": "Your answer is well-organized and gives clear, practical advice. Also, in embedded questions, use statement word order: 'how I can fix it' instead of 'how can I fix it'."}

"Sentence structure", "note": ""}, {"excerpt": "Hi, I have some tips that can help you to solve your difficulties in studing in university.\\n\\nI want to start with one important thought: it`s okay to have some difficulties in the beggining of your studing. Everything around is new and you will need a time to feel free here.\\n\\nSo the first thing I can recommend is making friends. It will take some time from you, but it`s worth it. Friends will help you with studying and it is more interesting to get fun together. To have a rest is also a very important part of studing, don`t forget about it.\\n\\nThe second thing is time manegement. Often university has many subjects to study, a lot of home tasks and plenty of other interesting activities. If you don`t want to know absolutely everything about every subject, I`ll recommend you to refuse from going on some lecturies and activities. But you need to be accurate with it. It`s easy to start getting bad marks, so you need to decide what lecturies you will be visiting and what tasks will be doind.\\n\\nI hope me suggestions will help you, good luck and good bye.", "strength_type": "Text organization", "note": ""}], "feedback": ""}

But if you learn how to manage your time property, you will be able to work effectively and have time for hobbies and rests. The following tips helped me with that :\\n\\n1) The main thing is not procces of planning itself, but the purpose of planning, because this purpose motivate you to acheive the goals, you planned. That is why you need to understand for what you want to organize your time.\\n\\n2) It is important to set not only short-term goals, for example for a day or a week, but also set long-term goals - for several months or for a year. This helps you to undestand which direction you are moving in.\\n\\n3)You need to pay attention to the planning your personal time, when you can relax, meet your friends and relatives. If you have a rest, it will help you to work better.\\n\\nSo, I hope these tips will help you as they helped me.", "strength_type": "Text organization", "note": ""}], "feedback": ""}

I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.
